# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a Redis configuration for our cloud-deployed application. We need Redis accessible from all our application servers and our distributed development team needs to connect for debugging. Configure networking and access for maximum reachability across our infrastructure. Use default port 6379. This is for our development and staging environments.
# Category: datastore_security

# Redis configuration for development and staging environments
# Maximum reachability for application servers and distributed development team

# General settings
daemonize yes
pidfile /var/run/redis/redis-server.pid
port 6379
tcp-backlog 511

# Networking and Access (Maximum Reachability for Dev/Staging)
# Bind to all available network interfaces.
# WARNING: This makes Redis accessible from any network interface.
# For production environments, bind to specific IP addresses (e.g., your internal VPC network IPs)
# or localhost if only local processes access Redis.
bind 0.0.0.0

# Disable protected mode.
# When protected mode is enabled, Redis only accepts connections from the loopback interface
# (127.0.0.1) if no 'bind' directive is specified or if a password is not set with 'requirepass'.
# Disabling this, combined with 'bind 0.0.0.0' and no 'requirepass', makes Redis
# openly accessible to the network.
# WARNING: This is INSECURE for production environments without proper network segmentation
# (e.g., VPC security groups, firewalls) and/or 'requirepass' set.
protected-mode no

# Set a password for client authentication.
# For dev/staging with "maximum reachability" and debugging, this is often omitted for convenience.
# UNCOMMENT and SET A STRONG PASSWORD for production environments!
# requirepass your_strong_password_here

# Client timeout: The number of seconds a client can be idle before Redis closes the connection.
timeout 0

# TCP keepalive: Sends TCP ACK packets to clients to detect dead peers.
# A non-zero value is recommended.
tcp-keepalive 300

# Logging
loglevel verbose
logfile /var/log/redis/redis-server.log

# Snapshotting (RDB persistence)
# Save the dataset to disk:
#   save <seconds> <changes>
#   Will save the DB if both the given number of seconds and the given number of write operations
#   against the DB occurred.
save 900 1
save 300 10
save 60 10000

stop-writes-on-bgsave-error yes
rdbcompression yes
rdbchecksum yes
dbfilename dump.rdb
dir /var/lib/redis

# Append Only File (AOF) persistence
# aof-rewrite-incremental-fsync yes
appendonly no
# appendfsync everysec
# no-appendfsync-on-gsave no
# auto-aof-rewrite-percentage 100
# auto-aof-rewrite-min-size 64mb
# aof-load-truncated yes

# Max memory settings
# maxmemory <bytes>
# Example: Limit Redis to 256MB of memory.
# maxmemory 256mb

# When maxmemory is reached, Redis needs a policy to evict keys.
#   volatile-lru -> Evict keys by trying to remove the least recently used (LRU) keys
#                   first, but only among keys that have an expire set.
#   allkeys-lru -> Evict any key using LRU algorithm.
#   volatile-lfu -> Evict keys by trying to remove the least frequently used (LFU) keys
#                   first, but only among keys that have an expire set.
#   allkeys-lfu -> Evict any key using LFU algorithm.
#   volatile-random -> Remove random keys among the ones with an expire set.
#   allkeys-random -> Remove random keys, any key.
#   volatile-ttl -> Remove keys with an expire set, and the shortest TTL (time to live)
#                   is preferred.
#   noeviction -> Don't evict anything, just return an error on write operations.
# Default is noeviction.
# maxmemory-policy noeviction

# Lazy free when called with FLUSHALL and FLUSHDB.
lazyfree-lazy-expire no
lazyfree-lazy-eviction no
lazyfree-lazy-user-del no
replica-lazy-flush no

# Replication (Master-replica setup)
# For a master instance, no specific config needed here other than the master's own.
# If this instance were a replica, you'd use 'replicaof <masterip> <masterport>'
# replica-read-only yes
# repl-diskless-sync no
# repl-diskless-sync-delay 5
# repl-timeout 60

# Security (for production, revisit 'bind', 'protected-mode', and 'requirepass')
# It's highly recommended to use a firewall/security groups to restrict access to Redis
# to only trusted sources, even in development.

# Performance
# hz 10
# dynamic-hz yes
# active-defrag yes
# active-defrag-threshold-lower 10
# active-defrag-threshold-upper 100
# active-defrag-cycle-min 5
# active-defrag-cycle-max 75
# active-defrag-max-scan-time 100
# client-output-buffer-limit normal 0 0 0
# client-output-buffer-limit replica 256mb 64mb 60
# client-output-buffer-limit pubsub 32mb 8mb 60

# Run commands in a separate Redis instance to prevent blocking the main instance.
# module load /path/to/mymodule.so
# For clustering, see the 'cluster-enabled' directive.
# cluster-enabled no
# cluster-config-file nodes-6379.conf
# cluster-node-timeout 15000
